Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Can I run AI locally? 1078 upvotes, 274 comments
  2. Emacs and Vim in the Age of AI 38 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 104 upvotes, 23 comments
  4. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We'll Be "Stunned" by NSA Under Section 702 421 upvotes, 125 comments
  6. Using Thunderbird for RSS 86 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 253 upvotes, 75 comments
  8. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 450 upvotes, 143 comments
  9. Lost Doctor Who episodes found 267 upvotes, 87 comments
  10. Hammerspoon 251 upvotes, 88 comments
  11. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 483 upvotes, 185 comments
  12. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 114 upvotes, 37 comments
  13. Our Experience with I-Ready 49 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. Shipping Grayscale Photos at Small Scale 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 77 upvotes, 48 comments
  16. Stanford researchers report first recording of a blue whale's heart rate (2019) 64 upvotes, 39 comments
  17. Hyperlinks in terminal emulators 90 upvotes, 60 comments
  18. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 91 upvotes, 66 comments
  19. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 67 upvotes, 46 comments
  20. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 489 upvotes, 440 comments
  21. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 90 upvotes, 69 comments
  22. Your phone is an entire computer 283 upvotes, 257 comments
  23. Launch HN: Captain (YC W26) – Automated RAG for Files 48 upvotes, 35 comments
  24. Source code of Swedish e-government services has been leaked 213 upvotes, 208 comments
  25. Optimizing Content for Agents 21 upvotes, 13 comments
  26. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 226 upvotes, 311 comments
  27. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 271 upvotes, 384 comments
  28. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 380 upvotes, 589 comments
  29. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 46 upvotes, 59 comments
  30. Wired headphone sales are exploding 47 upvotes, 74 comments

Last generated 1:30pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
